docs.title: How to use passGen to create 14-character passwords
docs.text:
passGen is a lightweight Windows app for creating randomly generated, 14-character passwords that contain:

  • Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers
  • Uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols

:material-download: Download passGen v3.1.0

For details about changes to the passGen app, see the release notes.

docs.title: Contributions
docs.text:
Did you encounter an error when using this app? Does this documentation need to be clarified, updated, or otherwise improved upon?

If so, pull requests are welcome! Please do the following:

  1. Open an issue on GitHub and describe what you would like to add or change.
  2. Create a pull request and link to your issue. This will help me track changes to the documentation and help provide context for future reference.

docs.title: How to create a randomly generated, 14-character password
docs.text: After downloading passGen, double-click the installation file to install the app. You can then choose which type of password to generate.

docs.title: Generate a password with symbols
docs.text:
To create a 14-character password containing symbols, numbers, uppercase letters, and lowercase letters, select Create a password with symbols.

Select Copy to copy the password.

Note

To see the password that you generated, select the asterisks masking the password.

docs.title: Generate a password without symbols
docs.text:
To create a 14-character password containing numbers, uppercase letters, and lowercase letters, select Create a password without symbols.

Select Copy to copy the password.

Note

To see the password that you generated, select the asterisks masking the password.

docs.title: Open multiple password windows
docs.text: If you need to generate multiple passwords and keep each window open, go to File and select Open new window to open a new window.

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Project forked from Password generator in C#.
  • Menu strip icons from Streamline Icons Free Pack License.

docs.title: How to uninstall passGen
docs.text:
To uninstall passGen, open Settings in Windows, then navigate to Add or remove programs.

Search for passGen in the program list, and click the three vertical dots, then select Uninstall.

Example

Uninstalling passGen

docs.title: How to update the app
docs.text:
When a new version of passGen is released, the app will prompt you to update. If you open the app and an update is available, the following screen will be displayed.

To update, select OK. Or if you want to skip the update, select Skip.

Warning

If you skip an update, you will not be prompted again until the next update. If you skipped an update and later decide to install it, you can download the newer version from Releases.
